jaletia-pancakes-of-corfu-argiro.name: Jaletia (pancakes) Of Corfu

ingredients.item: 50 % or more but not more than 50 %
1 Orange juicy its juice and zest
Honey or petimese or cinnamon with sugar
50 % or more but not more than 50 %
1 Orange juicy its juice and zest
Honey or petimese or cinnamon with sugar

instructions: In a bowl, add the flower and g.o.c.) yeast, zest, raisin and mix.
Add the honey, juice, water and salt.
With a spoon, mix the porridge to mix the ingredients.
Cover the bowl with membrane and allow the jaletia to puff slightly and bubbles on the surface for 40 minutes.
Heat some olive oil over medium heat in a pan.
Fry over medium heat for about 1.5 minutes, to brown one side and bubbles to look on the raw side.
Then turn the jams over and bake for 1 minute to brown on the other side. We take them out on Absorbent paper.
Add them to a platter and pour them with honey or petimese, or sprinkle a lot of cinnamon and sugar.
In many villages of Corfu they also make them without yeast, plain only with corn, water and rains.
In the end, they sprinkle themm with sugar and cinnamon.
In many houses, they are served with a seedful of kum quat that I love you.

jans-yummy-spaghetti.name: Jan's Yummy Spaghetti
instructions: Bring a pot of water to a boil; cook sausage in the boiling water until partially cooked, about 5 minutes. Remove sausage from water and slice.

Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook and stir ground turkey, onion, and garlic in the hot skillet until browned and crumbly, 5 to 7 minutes; drain and discard grease. Stir sausage, tomato sauce, chopped tomatoes, tomato paste, sugar, oregano, basil, and parsley into ground turkey; bring to a boil, stirring frequently. Reduce heat and simmer, stirring occasionally, until flavors have blended and sausage is fully cooked, about 1 hour.

Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook spaghetti in the boiling water, stirring occasionally until cooked through but firm to the bite, about 12 minutes. Drain and transfer spaghetti to a serving bowl; top with sauce. Toss to coat.
